Edge length of the unit cell (a) = 4 x 10-8cm

Radius of sodium atom (r)=?

q:
Given below are two statements. One is labelled as
Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.
Assertion A: Sharp glass edge becomes smooth on heating it up to its melting point.

Reason R: The viscosity of glass decreases on melting.

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
question:
Given below are two statements. One is labelled as
Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.
Assertion A: Sharp glass edge becomes smooth on heating it up to its melting point.

Reason R: The viscosity of glass decreases on melting.

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is true but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is false

Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct
explanation of A.
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is false but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is true.

Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> are true and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>.
solution: Sharp glass edges are heated to make them smooth. Because on heating glass melts and the surface of the liquid tends to take the rounded shape at the edges which has minimum surface area. This is called fire polishing of glass. Correct explanation for this is surface tension.

q: The correct relationships between unit cell edge length ‘<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math>’ and radius of sphere ‘<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi></math>’ for face-centred and body-centred cubic structures respectively are:
question: The correct relationships between unit cell edge length ‘<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math>’ and radius of sphere ‘<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi></math>’ for face-centred and body-centred cubic structures respectively are:
solution:
A face-centered cubic (fcc) unit cell contains a component in the center of each face in addition to those at the corners of the cube. The atoms touch each other along the face diagonal.

For  FCC:  a2=4r

a=22r

A body-centered cubic (bcc) unit cell contains a component in the center of the cube in addition to those at the corners of the cube. The atoms touch each other along the body diagonal.

For BCC : a3=4r

q: Which of the following arrangements shows the schematic alignment of magnetic moments of antiferromagnetic substance?
question: Which of the following arrangements shows the schematic alignment of magnetic moments of antiferromagnetic substance?
solution: Substances which are expected to possess para-magnetic or ferromagnetism on the basis of unpaired electrons, but actually they possess zero net magnetic moment are called anti-ferromagnetic substance.

subject: Chemistry
q: Na and Mg crystallize in BCC and FCC type crystals respectively, then the number of atoms of Na and Mg present in the unit cell of their respective crystal is
question: Na and Mg crystallize in BCC and FCC type crystals respectively, then the number of atoms of Na and Mg present in the unit cell of their respective crystal is
solution:
BCC - points are at corners and one in the centre of the unit cell
Number of atoms per unit cell $=8 \times \frac{1}{8}+1=2$
FCC - points are at the corners and also centre of the six faces of each cell
Number of atoms per unit cell $=8 \times \frac{1}{8}+6 \times \frac{1}{2}=4$

8 chloride ions at 8 corners Cs + cation at body centre along body diagonal there will be one Cs + cation and 2  Cl − ions at ends of body diagonal.
2 r Cs + + 2 r Cl - = 3 a
a is edge length

q: Ionic radii of cation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>+</mo></msup></math> and anion <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>-</mo></msup></math> are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>102</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>181</mn><mi>pm</mi></math> respectively. This crystal has cubic close packing for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>-</mo></msup><mo>.</mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>+</mo></msup></math> is present in all octahedral voids. The edge length of the unit cell of the crystal <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>AB</mi></math> is___<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>pm</mi></math>.
question: Ionic radii of cation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>+</mo></msup></math> and anion <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>-</mo></msup></math> are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>102</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>181</mn><mi>pm</mi></math> respectively. These ions are allowed to crystallize into an ionic solid. This crystal has cubic close packing for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>-</mo></msup><mo>.</mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>+</mo></msup></math> is present in all octahedral voids. The edge length of the unit cell of the crystal <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>AB</mi></math> is___<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>pm</mi></math>.
solution:
In given ionic compound anions form CCP lattice, And cation is present in octahedral void. Means this structure is similar to structure of NaCl. Which is also called rock salt structure.

Edge length = a=?

In rock salt structure cations and anions are in contact along the edge of the cube. 

So, a=2r++r-

a=2102+181

a=2283

a=566 pm

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following compounds is likely to show both Frenkel and Schottky defects in its crystalline form?
question: Which of the following compounds is likely to show both Frenkel and Schottky defects in its crystalline form?
solution:
AgBr can exhibit both Schottky and Frenkel defect.

Schottky defect in AgBr is exhibited due to the precipitation of both cations and anions. In AgBr, Ag+ ion is small in size and when removed from the lattice point, they can occupy the interstitial sites and, hence, shows both Frenkel and Schottky defects.

q: A cubic solid is made up of two elements <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math>. Atoms of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> are present on every alternate corner and one at the center of cube. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> is at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac><mi>rd</mi></msup></math> of the total faces. The empirical formula of the compound is
question: A cubic solid is made up of two elements <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math>. Atoms of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> are present on every alternate corner and one at the center of cube. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> is at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac><mi>rd</mi></msup></math> of the total faces. The empirical formula of the compound is
solution:
The share of the atom present at corner of the cube is 1/8. The share of the atom at face centre is 1/2. Hence, the molecule can be represented as follows,

X4×18+1×1Y6×13×12

⇒X12+1  Y1

⇒X23  Y1

⇒X1.5  Y1(Empirical formula)

⇒X3  Y2.

The number of carbon atoms present per unit cell of diamond is ________.
question: Diamond has a three dimensional structure of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> atoms formed by covalent bonds. The structure of diamond has face centred cubic lattice where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo>%</mo></math> of the tetrahedral voids are also occupied by carbon atoms. The number of carbon atoms present per unit cell of diamond is ________.
solution:
Carbon atoms occupy FCC lattice points as well as half of the tetrahedral voids

therefore number of carbon atoms atoms per unit cell =8

q: Which of the following expressions is correct in case of a <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CsCl</mi></math> unit cell (edge length ‘<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math>’)<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>?</mo></math>

solution:
CsCl has body centered type structure in which Cs+ occupies at corner of a cube and Cl– occupies the centre of the cube.

2rCs++2rCl-=3a (where a is the edge length of the cube)

rCs++rCr=32a

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I : Frenkel defects are vacancy as well as interstitial defects.
Statement II : Frenkel defect leads to colour in ionic solids due to presence of F-centres.

Choose the most appropriate answer for the statements from the options given below:
question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I : Frenkel defects are vacancy as well as interstitial defects.
Statement II : Frenkel defect leads to colour in ionic solids due to presence of F-centres.

Choose the most appropriate answer for the statements from the options given below:
options: Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
solution: A Frenkel defect is another form of a point defect which is created when an atom or cation leaves its original place in the lattice structure to create a vacancy while occupying another interstitial position within the solid crystal. Frenkel defect is dislocation defect, it is not interstitial defect, and it is not responsible for colour of solid.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Iron oxide FeO, crystallises in a cubic lattice with a unit cell edge length of 5.0Å. If density of the FeO in the crystal is 4.0 g cm-3, then the number of FeO units present per unit cell is _____ (Nearest integer)

Given : Molar mass of Fe and O is 56 and 16 g mol-1 respectively.
NA=6.0×1023 mol-1
question:
Iron oxide FeO, crystallises in a cubic lattice with a unit cell edge length of 5.0Å. If density of the FeO in the crystal is 4.0 g cm-3, then the number of FeO units present per unit cell is _____ (Nearest integer)

Given : Molar mass of Fe and O is 56 and 16 g mol-1 respectively.
NA=6.0×1023 mol-1
solution:
The density of the crystal can be calculated as follows,

d=Z×MN0×a3

d=densityZ=effective number of atomsM=Molar massN0=Avogadro's numbera=Edge length

4=Z×726×1023×125×10-24

Z=4.166≃4

question: The total number of octahedral void (s) per atom present in a cubic close packed structure is :

In CCP there are four atoms per unit cell octahedral voids are

12Edge centres14+1Body centre=4 O.V. [Contribution at edge centre is14 ]

subject: Chemistry
q: All of the following share the same crystal structure except.
question: All of the following share the same crystal structure except.
solution:
$\mathrm{RbCl}, \mathrm{NaCl}$ and $\mathrm{CsCl}$ share the same crystal structure except $\mathrm{LiCl}$.
$\mathrm{LiCl}$ is deliquescent. It crystalises as a hydrated LiCl. $2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$. Other alkali metal chlorides do not form hydrates.

subject: Chemistry
q: The incorrect statement about the imperfections in solids is
question: The incorrect statement about the imperfections in solids is
options: Schottky defect decreases the density of the substance.
Frenkel defect does not alter the density of the substance.
Interstitial defect increases the density of the substance.
Vacancy defect increases the density of the substance.
solution:
Frenkel Defect: This defect is shown by ionic solids. The smaller ion (usually cation) is dislocated from its normal site to an interstitial site. It creates a vacancy defect at its original site and an interstitial defect at its new location. Frenkel defect is also called dislocation defect. It does not change the density of the solid. In all other defects there is a change in the density.

In Schottky defect equal number of cation and anion are missing from their position so, it decreases the density of the substance.

In Interstitial defect extra atoms are present at interstitial position so, it increases the density of the substance.

Due to vacancy defect density of the substance will decrease. since atoms are missing from their positions.

subject: Chemistry
q: The ratio of number of atoms present in a simple cubic, body centered cubic and face centered cubic structure are, respectively :
question: The ratio of number of atoms present in a simple cubic, body centered cubic and face centered cubic structure are, respectively :
solution:
ZSC = 8 × 18= 1 

[Atoms are only at the corners]

ZBCC = 8 × 18 +1= 1 + 1=2 

[Atoms are at the corners and at the body center]

ZFCC = 8 × 18 + 6 × 12= 1 + 3 = 4  

[Atoms are at the corners and at the face centers]

Here, Z= effective number of atoms in the unit cell.

Therefore, ZSC​: ZBCC​: ZFCC​=1 : 2 : 4

 




SC:BCC:FCC=1:2:4

subject: Chemistry
q: The appearance of colour in solid alkali metal halides is generally due to:
question: The appearance of colour in solid alkali metal halides is generally due to:
solution:
The appearance of colour in alkali metal halides is generally due to F-centres. F centers in $\mathrm{NaCl}$ produce a band of optical absorption towards the blue end of visible
spectrum, thus the colour appears yellow under transmitted light. On the other hand, $\mathrm{KCl}$ with $\mathrm{F}$-centers appears magenta, and $\mathrm{KBr}$ appears blue.

subject: Chemistry
q: The empirical formula for a compound with a cubic close packed arrangement of anions and with cations occupying all the octahedral sites in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></msub><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>.</mo></math> The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></math> is (Integer answer)
question: The empirical formula for a compound with a cubic close packed arrangement of anions and with cations occupying all the octahedral sites in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></msub><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>.</mo></math> The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></math> is (Integer answer)
solution:
In AxB
Effective number of B atoms =4 (in CCP)
Effective number of A atoms =4 (all O.V.)
So formula of the compound =A4B4=AB
So x=1

subject: Chemistry
q:
A diatomic molecule X2 has a body-centred cubic (bcc) structure with a cell edge of 300 pm. The density of the molecule is 6.17 gcm-3. The number of molecules present in 200 g of X2 is :
(Avogadro constant NA=6×1023mol-1)
question:
A diatomic molecule X2 has a body-centred cubic (bcc) structure with a cell edge of 300 pm. The density of the molecule is 6.17 gcm-3. The number of molecules present in 200 g of X2 is :
(Avogadro constant NA=6×1023mol-1)
solution:
For BCC z=2

d=Z×MNA× Volume 

6.17=2×M6.02×1023×3×10-83

6.17=2×M6.02×2.7

M=50

No. of mole =20050=4

No. of molecule =4 NA

q: Which of the following exists as covalent crystals in the solid state ?
question: Which of the following exists as covalent crystals in the solid state ?
solution:
Silicon is covalent or network solid.

Each Si atom is in sp3 hybridisation.

Sulphur (S8)  –  molecular solid.

Phosphorus (P4)  – molecular solid.

Iodine (I2)   –  molecular solid.

q: Which one of the following statements about packing in solids is incorrect ?
question: Which one of the following statements about packing in solids is incorrect ?
options: Coordination number in bcc mode of packing is 8 .
Coordination number in hep mode of packing is 12 .
Void space in hcp mode of packing is $32 \%$.
Void space in ccp mode of packing is $26 \%$.
solution: The $h c p$ arrangement of atoms occupies $74 \%$ of the available space and thus has $26 \%$ vacant space.

subject: Chemistry
q: In a face centered cubic lattice atoms A are at the corner points and atoms B at the face centered points. If atom B is missing from one of the face centered points, the formula of the ionic compound is :
question: In a face centered cubic lattice atoms A are at the corner points and atoms B at the face centered points. If atom B is missing from one of the face centered points, the formula of the ionic compound is :
solution:
A atoms at corners  = 8 × 1 8 =  A (one atom)

B atom is missing at One face centre then will present at 5 faces

At face centre atom contribute 1/2 part

Number of B atom at Face centre = 5× 1 2 (B atom)

So formula of compound = A2B5

subject: Chemistry
q: In a face centred cubic lattice, atoms of A form the corner points and atoms of B form the face centred points. If two atoms of $\mathrm{A}$ are missing from the corner points, the formula of the ionic compound is :
question: In a face centred cubic lattice, atoms of A form the corner points and atoms of B form the face centred points. If two atoms of $\mathrm{A}$ are missing from the corner points, the formula of the ionic compound is :
solution:
A form corner points and two atoms of A are missing from corner
$$
\begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad & \text { Atoms at corner }(\mathrm{A})=6 \times \frac{1}{8}=\frac{3}{4} \\
& \text { Atoms at face centre }(\mathrm{B})=6 \times \frac{1}{2}=3 \\
\therefore & \mathrm{A}_{3 / 4} \mathrm{~B}_3 \text { i.e., } \mathrm{AB}_4
\end{aligned}
$$

subject: Chemistry
q:
A hard substance melts at high temperature and is an insulator in both solid and in molten state.
This solid is most likely to be a / an :
question:
A hard substance melts at high temperature and is an insulator in both solid and in molten state.
This solid is most likely to be a / an :
solution: Covalent or network solid have very high melting point, and they are insulators in their solid and molten form.
